title: genista acanthoclada subsp. echinus
intro: Genista acanthoclada subsp. echinus, commonly known as a subalpine genista, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family, Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues and digestive problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Genista acanthoclada subsp. echinus include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, where it is dried and combined with other herbs to create remedies for various health conditions.
